user_message: How should I coach David Chen on Blue Yonder Airlines? There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 09:10 EST
Subject: Follow-up: partner engagement Review

Our team encountered some challenges with partner engagement. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 08:31 EST
Subject: Re: Re: partner engagement Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-02-12
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into partner engagement.

[00:07:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:21:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:27:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:29:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:32:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-L trap (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-L failure mode (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-L -- Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
